industries.name: Radioluminescent Devices & Nuclear Batteries
uses: Promethium-147 (¹⁴⁷Pm, t½ = 2.62 years, beta emitter) used in radioluminescent paint for aircraft instrument dials, watch hands, and military equipment — replaced radium-226 due to shorter half-life and lower gamma radiation

Betavoltaic nuclear batteries: ¹⁴⁷Pm beta particles slow in a semiconductor junction (silicon or gallium arsenide), generating a small but extremely long-lived electrical current — proposed for pacemakers, remote sensors, and spacecraft where battery replacement is impossible

Promethium X-ray source: ¹⁴⁷Pm beta particles striking a target produce low-energy bremsstrahlung X-rays for thickness gauging of thin materials (paper, plastic film, metal foil) in manufacturing quality control
Promethium is entirely synthetic — no stable isotopes exist. All ¹⁴⁷Pm is produced by neutron irradiation of neodymium-146 in nuclear reactors, or recovered from uranium fission product streams

companies.name: Oak Ridge National Laboratory
companies.use: Primary US producer of promethium-147 via neutron irradiation in its High Flux Isotope Reactor (HFIR). Supplies ¹⁴⁷Pm for research, calibration sources, and specialty applications to government and commercial customers.

companies.name: Nuclear Batteries Inc. / BetaBatt
companies.use: Developing betavoltaic nuclear batteries using ¹⁴⁷Pm and other beta emitters for ultra-long-life power sources for implantable medical devices, remote environmental sensors, and space applications where decades of operation without maintenance are required.

industries.name: Industrial Gauging & Measurement
uses: Promethium-147 thickness gauges: beta radiation from ¹⁴⁷Pm is attenuated by material thickness — used in non-contact measurement of paper, plastic film, rubber sheet, and thin metal foil thickness in continuous manufacturing processes
Luminous paints for emergency exit signs and aircraft instrument backlighting — ¹⁴⁷Pm-activated zinc sulfide phosphor produces continuous green glow without external power source

Static elimination: ¹⁴⁷Pm ionizes air near charged surfaces, neutralizing static electricity in paper, plastic film, and textile manufacturing — a niche but reliable application in sensitive manufacturing environments

companies.name: Thermo Fisher Scientific
companies.use: Supplies ¹⁴⁷Pm calibration sources and thickness gauge sources for industrial measurement applications. Also a major supplier of other beta and gamma radiation sources for industrial process control and laboratory use.
